Theorem 4.3.9.

Suppose that \(\bff(x)\) is a vector-valued function, differentiable on \((a, b)\) and continuous on \([a, b]\text{,}\) and that there exists some \(M\gt 0\) such that \(\Vert \bff'(x)\Vert \le M\) for all \(x\in (a, b)\text{.}\) Then \(\Vert \bff(b)-\bff(a)\Vert \le M(b-a)\text{.}\)

Proof.

in-context
